prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 4 working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-bJrBsV.

Your task: Implement an email notification system using the EventBus pattern.

1. Create or extend src/services/notification.service.ts:
   - Use an EventBus (publish/subscribe) pattern for dispatching notifications
   - Implement an email channel handler that listens to the EventBus
   - Events should carry: type, recipient, subject, body

2. Create src/utils/event-bus.ts (if it does not already exist):
   - Simple in-process EventBus with subscribe(event, handler) and publish(event, data)

3. Add at least one unit test for the notification service

Keep the design extensible — additional channel types will be added later.
Follow existing repository patterns and use dependency injection via constructor parameters.

parameters.task: Implement email notification system using EventBus pattern: create src/utils/event-bus.ts, extend src/services/notification.service.ts with pub/sub notification dispatching, add unit tests

parameters.prompt:
Explore the TaskFlow Pro codebase at /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-bJrBsV. I need to understand:

1. The overall directory structure (list key directories and files)
2. Read src/services/ - what services exist and what patterns do they follow (dependency injection, etc.)
3. Read src/utils/ - what utilities exist
4. Check if there's already an event-bus.ts or notification.service.ts
5. Look at existing test files to understand the testing patterns used
6. Check tsconfig.json and package.json for project setup

Be thorough - read the actual file contents of existing services and tests so I understand the patterns. Report file contents concisely.

parameters.new_string:
export { NotificationService, EmailNotificationChannel } from './services/notification.service.js';
export type { NotificationEvent, NotificationChannel } from './services/notification.service.js';

parameters.summary: Use internal SimpleEventBus for dispatching notifications to channel handlers (email, etc.)
parameters.context: Need to add email notification support with an extensible channel-based design. The existing NotificationService stores notifications in-memory but has no concept of delivery channels.
parameters.rationale: A separate SimpleEventBus (src/utils/event-bus.ts) with subscribe/publish decouples the notification service from specific delivery mechanisms. Channels implement a NotificationChannel interface and subscribe to the internal bus. This keeps the existing app-level EventBus (src/events/event-bus.ts) for domain events while using a lightweight bus for notification dispatch. New channels (SMS, push, etc.) can be added by implementing NotificationChannel without modifying NotificationService.

alternatives.option: Add channels directly to NotificationService without an internal EventBus
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tight coupling — adding new channels would require modifying the service class each time

alternatives.option: Reuse the existing app-level EventBus for notification dispatch
alternatives.reason_rejected: Conflates domain events with notification delivery events; different concerns should have separate buses

diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing
